description.content: Endpoints for listing currently firing and pending alerts.
Returns a list of all active alerts currently firing or pending in Prometheus. Alerts include their labels, state, and timestamps.
Endpoints for querying label names, label values, series metadata, and metric metadata without executing PromQL expressions.
Returns a list of label names present in the TSDB. Optionally filtered by a time range or series selectors. Useful for building autocomplete in query UIs.

query.description: Start of the time range filter.
End of the time range filter.
Series selector(s) to restrict the label results.
Series selector(s) to restrict the label results.
Maximum number of items to return.

description.content: Returns a list of values for a given label name. Optionally filtered by a time range or series selectors. The special label name __name__ returns all metric names.

query.description: Start of the time range filter.
End of the time range filter.
Series selector(s) to restrict the label value results.
Series selector(s) to restrict the label value results.
Maximum number of items to return.

variable.description: (Required) The label name to retrieve values for.
(Required) The label name to retrieve values for.
(Required) The label name to retrieve values for.
(Required) The label name to retrieve values for.

description.content: Returns the list of time series that match a certain label set. The response contains the label sets identifying each time series. At least one match[] selector must be provided.

query.description: (Required) Series selector(s). At least one required.
(Required) Series selector(s). At least one required.
Start of the time range filter.
End of the time range filter.
Maximum number of items to return.

description.content: Returns metadata about metrics currently scraped from targets. Metadata includes the metric type (counter, gauge, histogram, summary, untyped) and help string. Multiple targets may provide metadata for the same metric.

query.description: Maximum number of items to return.
Metric name to filter by. If omitted, all metrics are returned.
Maximum number of metadata entries to return per metric.

description.content: PromQL instant and range query endpoints for evaluating expressions against the time series database.
Evaluates a PromQL expression at a single point in time. Returns a vector of time series with their values at the specified time. If the time parameter is omitted, the current server time is used.

query.description: (Required) PromQL expression to evaluate.
Evaluation timestamp as a Unix timestamp or RFC3339 string. Defaults to current server time.
Evaluation timeout as a duration string (e.g., 30s).

response.name: Bad request — invalid query expression or parameters
Unprocessable entity — expression cannot be executed
Query timed out or was aborted

description.content: Evaluates a PromQL expression at a single point in time using a POST request body. Use POST when query parameters exceed URL length limits.
(Required) PromQL expression to evaluate.
Evaluation timestamp.
Evaluation timeout.
(Required) PromQL expression to evaluate.
Evaluation timestamp.
Evaluation timeout.
Evaluates a PromQL expression over a range of time, returning a matrix of time series with values at each step interval. Used for generating graphs and time-based analysis.

query.description: (Required) PromQL expression to evaluate.
(Required) Start of the time range as a Unix timestamp or RFC3339 string.
(Required) End of the time range as a Unix timestamp or RFC3339 string.
(Required) Query resolution step width as a duration string (e.g., 15s, 1m, 1h).
Evaluation timeout as a duration string (e.g., 30s).

response.name: Bad request — invalid query or time parameters

description.content: Evaluates a PromQL expression over a range of time using a POST request body. Use POST when query parameters exceed URL length limits.

Returns a list of exemplars for a given PromQL expression over a time range. Exemplars are specific trace or log IDs attached to high-value metric observations that link metrics to traces.

query.description: (Required) PromQL expression to evaluate.
(Required) Start of the time range.
(Required) End of the time range.

description.content: Endpoints for retrieving loaded recording rules and alerting rules.
Returns a list of alerting and recording rules currently loaded. Rules include the rule expression, their last evaluation time, and any active alerts for alerting rules.

query.description: Filter by rule type.
Filter by rule name.
Filter by rule name.
Filter by rule group name.
Filter by rule group name.
Filter by the file the rule group was loaded from.
Filter by the file the rule group was loaded from.

response.name: Rules returned successfully

description.content: Endpoints for retrieving Prometheus server configuration, flags, build information, and TSDB statistics.
Returns an overview of the current state of the Prometheus Alertmanager service discovery including active and dropped Alertmanager instances.
Returns the currently loaded configuration file as a YAML string. Includes the full Prometheus configuration including scrape configs, rule files, and alerting configuration.
Returns the values of all command-line flag settings currently in use by the Prometheus server.
Returns build and version information for the Prometheus server, including version, revision, branch, build user, build date, and Go version.
Returns various runtime properties about the Prometheus server including the start time, storage retention, TSDB stats summary, and corruptible chunk count.
Returns the current status of the WAL (Write-Ahead Log) replay. WAL replay occurs during Prometheus startup to recover data from the most recent checkpoint.
Endpoints for discovering scrape targets and their current health status.
Returns an overview of the current state of the Prometheus target discovery including active and dropped targets, their labels, and scrape health status.

query.description: Filter targets by state.

description.content: Returns metadata about metrics for individual targets. Allows filtering by target label matchers and metric name to retrieve per-target metadata contributed by scrape pools.

query.description: Label selectors matching target labels.
Metric name to filter by.
Maximum number of items to return.

description.content: Endpoints for querying TSDB statistics and deleting time series data.
Deletes data for a selection of series in a time range. This only creates tombstones in the TSDB; actual deletion happens on the next compaction. Requires --web.enable-admin-api flag.

query.description: (Required) Series selector(s) to delete. At least one required.
(Required) Series selector(s) to delete. At least one required.
Start of the time range. Defaults to minimum possible time.
End of the time range. Defaults to maximum possible time.

description.content: Returns a set of properties about the Prometheus TSDB including the top series by label count, top chunks by label, most frequently changing metrics, and head statistics.

query.description: Maximum number of items to return.

description.content: Prometheus server hostname
Prometheus server port

The Prometheus HTTP API provides endpoints for executing instant and range queries using PromQL, querying metadata such as label names, label values, and series, and accessing targets, rules, alerts, and TSDB information. The API is reachable under /api/v1 on a Prometheus server and returns JSON responses with a consistent envelope containing status, data, and optional error fields.
